ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chicken nuggets as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared fast-food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 10-piece serving of chicken nuggets weighs approximately 170 g, representing a typical fast-food portion for one person.

nutrient_source.title: 10Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Original — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '10Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Original' (cr_id G00010021). All core macros and available micronutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for breaded chicken nuggets (USDA SR Legacy #05298) as HPB does not report these fields for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: breaded and fried chicken nuggets, which share a refined wheat-flour breading matrix. Reference: Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283, University of Sydney GI Database entry for fried breaded chicken products. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 66 × (14.3 × 1.70) / 100 = 66 × 24.31 / 100 ≈ 16.0 / 100 × 170g portion → GL = (66 × 14.3 / 100) × 1.70 = 9.44 × 0.65 ≈ 6.2 per 170 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of these chicken nuggets, as a single 170 g serving delivers approximately 1,204 mg sodium — more than half the recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — and the potassium content of 250 mg/100 g is insufficient to meaningfully offset this sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-flour breading raises postprandial glucose; high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid these nuggets because the refined breading coating contributes a medium glycemic index (~66) and the high total fat with trans fat content worsens insulin resistance; grilled or baked skinless chicken is a clinically preferable alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.6 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (5.2 g/100 g), trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), and very high sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of these deep-fried breaded nuggets, as the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium contributes to dyslipidaemia, arterial stiffness, and elevated blood pressure — all established cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat and saturated fat are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets, as the AGE load from high-temperature frying and the presence of trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) and saturated fat are known to amplify systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 10-Piece Chicken Nuggets Original is a deep-fried, breaded fast-food chicken product sold in Singapore, classified under the HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010021) as a chicken-based dish. Each nugget consists of minced or formed chicken meat coated in a refined wheat-flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy golden exterior and a moist, tender interior. The product is halal-certified and commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner item, often paired with dipping sauces. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g fat, and 14.3 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notably high at 708 mg/100 g, exceeding the 600 mg/100 g threshold that flags a food as unsuitable for hypertension management. The estimated trans fat content of 0.3 g/100 g and saturated fat of approximately 5.2 g/100 g raise cardiovascular and inflammatory concerns. The refined breading contributes a medium glycemic index (~66), making postprandial glucose management challenging for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Deep-frying at high temperatures generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory and of particular concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or metabolic syndrome. This product is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or inflammatory conditions. Grilled or baked skinless chicken alternatives are strongly preferred in clinical dietary guidance.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in chicken nuggets?
Are chicken nuggets suitable for hypertension?
Fast food chicken nuggets fat content

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for an exact named product match (cr_id G00010021). Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA SR Legacy for breaded chicken nuggets, reducing those specific fields to medium confidence. Overall record confidence is high given the primary source is an approved canonical Singapore food database.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this product, and no equivalent Singapore-specific approved source provides this breakdown. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product; USDA SR Legacy (#05298) does not provide a complete micronutrient panel for this exact formulation. trans_fat_g set to 0.3 based on estimation consistent with deep-fried breaded chicken products per USDA Branded Food data for comparable products; this is an estimate and should be verified against the manufacturer's specification. saturated_fat_g of 5.2 g/100 g and dietary_fibre_g of 0.8 g/100 g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for breaded fried chicken nuggets (closest approved-source analogue) as HPB does not report these fields for this item. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for breaded fried chicken products; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in any approved source. All core macros (protein_g 16.5, fat_g 22.6, carbohydrate_g 14.3) are non-zero and sourced from HPB at confidence 0.95; no zero-macro justification is required.
